prompt: My late arrival caused the professor considerable ______.
explanation: Target: upset. Disturbance or disruption.

prompt: Every once in a while, though, Idol comes across an ______ talent who just blows everyone away.
explanation: Target: unusual. Not usual, out of the ordinary

prompt: He's going for his ______ check-up at the hospital.
explanation: Target: weekly. Happening once a week, or every week.

prompt: Except for one or two days a year, he ______ walks to work.
explanation: Target: usually. Most of the time; less than always, but more than occasionally.

prompt: The reasons are ______.
explanation: Target: various. Having a broad range (of different elements).

prompt: After mending the lion's leg, we returned him to the ______.
explanation: Target: wild. The undomesticated state of a wild animal

prompt: On a recent windy day, hundreds of visitors climbed ______ stairs to take pictures in front of the glacier.
explanation: Target: wooden. Made of wood.

prompt: ______ is a good way to go.
explanation: Target: up. The direction opposed to the pull of gravity.

prompt: Injustice is a heinous ______.
explanation: Target: wrong. Something that is immoral or not good.

prompt: He has never ______ been late for an appointment;   I’m not yet wise enough to answer that;   Have you finished yet?
explanation: Target: yet. (usually with negative) Thus far; up to the present; up to some specified time; still

prompt: There is one sterling and here is ______
explanation: Target: another. An additional one of the same kind.

prompt: It is hard work to swim ______ the current.
explanation: Target: against. In a contrary direction to.

prompt: A small table ______ the bed
explanation: Target: beside. Next to; at the side of.

prompt: I want to buy one that costs somewhere ______ forty and fifty dollars.
explanation: Target: between. In the position or interval that separates (two things), or intermediate in quantity or degree. (See Usage notes below.)

prompt: you ______ smoke outside;  may I sit there?
explanation: Target: may. (modal auxiliary verb, defective) To have permission to, be allowed. Used in granting permission and in questions to make polite requests.

prompt: He pushed with all his ______, but still it would not move.
explanation: Target: might. Physical strength or force.

prompt: If you'll be out all day, a map is a ______.
explanation: Target: must. Something that is mandatory or required.

prompt: a ______ number
explanation: Target: minus. Being a negative quantity; pertaining to a deficit or reduction.

prompt: The door closed by ______
explanation: Target: itself. (reflexive pronoun) it; A thing as the object of a verb or preposition that also appears as the subject

prompt: I taught ______.
explanation: Target: myself. (reflexive pronoun) Me, as direct or indirect object the speaker as the object of a verb or preposition, when the speaker is also the subject.

prompt: Whether you're a ______ or a player, everyone in Tinseltown wants to be King of Hollywood! - Conquering Hollywood
explanation: Target: nobody. Someone who is not important or well-known.

prompt: We went to the store but ______ was there.
explanation: Target: no one. Used in contrast to anyone, someone or everyone: not one person; nobody.

prompt: We should keep this for ______.
explanation: Target: ourselves. (reflexive pronoun) Us; the group including the speaker as the object of a verb or preposition when that group also is the subject.

prompt: a book about a time machine that can transport people back into the ______
explanation: Target: past. The period of time that has already happened, in contrast to the present and the future.
Target: per. For each.

prompt: He is a real ______ to the team.
explanation: Target: plus. An asset or useful addition.

prompt: “______!” said the girl after her mom gave her a gift.
explanation: Target: thank you. An expression of gratitude or politeness in response to something done or given.

prompt: I hope that we ______ win the game.
explanation: Target: shall. (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.

prompt: Interstate highways form a nationwide system of ______ roads.
explanation: Target: through. Passing from one side of something to the other.

prompt: It looks weaker and weaker, ______ more I think about it.
explanation: Target: the. With a comparative or with more and a verb phrase, establishes a correlation with one or more other such comparatives.

prompt: A short/long time ______
explanation: Target: since. From a specified time in the past.

prompt: A player ______ whom none is more skillful.
explanation: Target: than. Introduces a comparison, and is associated with comparatives, and with words such as more, less, and fewer. Typically, it seeks to measure the force of an adjective or similar description between two predicates.

prompt: I have to work ______ eight o'clock tonight.
explanation: Target: till. Until; to, up to; as late as (a given time).

prompt: I'm tired of being a nobody – I want to be a ______.
explanation: Target: somebody. A recognised or important person, a celebrity.

prompt: Do you need a gift for that special ______?
explanation: Target: someone. A partially specified but unnamed person.

prompt: They are going to try climbing Mount Everest ______.
explanation: Target: themselves. The reflexive case of they, the third-person plural personal pronoun. The group of people, animals or objects previously mentioned, as the object of a verb or following a preposition (also used for emphasis).

prompt: Do we need ______ many recommendations?
explanation: Target: this. To the degree or extent indicated.

prompt: He did? That's a ______!
explanation: Target: wow. Anything exceptionally surprising, unbelievable, outstanding, etc.

prompt: ______! We did it!
explanation: Target: yeah. Expressing joy, celebration, glee, etc.

prompt: We entered the house and found a ready ______.
explanation: Target: welcome. Kind reception of a guest or newcomer.

prompt: Do what you ______.
explanation: Target: will. (now uncommon or literary) To wish, desire (something).

prompt: Ensure the patient is sufficiently ______.
explanation: Target: under. Under anesthesia, especially general anesthesia; sedated.

prompt: Being from a large, poor family, he learned to live ______.
explanation: Target: without. Lacking something.

prompt: If you can wait ______ after my meeting with her, we'll talk then.
explanation: Target: until. Up to the time of (something happening).

prompt: Do you want to come ______?
explanation: Target: with. Along, together with others, in a group, etc.

prompt: By now, you must surely know ______ is which.
explanation: Target: which. (interrogative) What one or ones (of those mentioned or implied).

prompt: I don't know ______ it is. (indirect question)
explanation: Target: who. (interrogative) What person or people; which person or people; asks for the identity of someone. (used in a direct or indirect question)

prompt: Both of ______ should get ready now.
explanation: Target: you. (object pronoun) The people spoken, or written to, as an object.

prompt: If this edit is mine, the other must be ______.  Their encyclopedia is good, but yours is even better.  It’s all yours.
explanation: Target: yours. That which belongs to you (singular); the possessive second-person singular pronoun used without a following noun.

prompt: Be careful with that fire or you'll burn ______.
explanation: Target: yourself. (reflexive pronoun) Your own self (singular).

prompt: Could you move that up ______?
explanation: Target: a bit. To a small extent; in a small amount; rather.

prompt: They don't make repairable radios ______.
explanation: Target: any more. (in negative or interrogative constructions) From a given time onwards; longer, again.

prompt: ______ the obvious financial benefits, the merger will allow us some breathing room with regards to R&D.
explanation: Target: as well as. In addition to; further to.

prompt: Please ______ and look around.
explanation: Target: come in. To enter.

prompt: He's a reasonable man. I knew he'd ______ eventually.
explanation: Target: come round. To change one's opinion to a prevailing one.

prompt: ______ everyone can make a movie using their mobile phone, which we didn't use to be able to do.
explanation: Target: these days. Currently, at present.

prompt: ______ I shall upgrade my software, but not just yet.
explanation: Target: one day. At some unspecified time in the future.

prompt: Jack and Robert loved ______.
explanation: Target: each other. (reciprocal pronoun) To one another; one to the other; signifies that a verb applies to two or more entities both as subjects and as direct objects:

prompt: Dawn believes in ______, but Willow believes in multiple gods and goddesses.
explanation: Target: god. The single deity of various monotheistic religions, especially the deity of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.

prompt: But imagine, ______, a world where climate change did not exist.
explanation: Target: if you like. Please, kindly; used in giving a non-concrete instruction.

prompt: What do you want to be when you ______?
explanation: Target: grow up. To mature and become an adult.

prompt: I'm going to ______ that I get to the interview on time.
explanation: Target: make sure. To ensure; to take care that something happens.

prompt: Did you fall over and hurt your knee? ______, I’ll put a bandage on it.
choices: put on sth or put sth on
explanation: Target: never mind. It is not important; do not fret; used to reassure or comfort the person to whom it is said.

prompt: Is much ______ with the old plan?
explanation: Target: the matter. Wrong

prompt: I was talking to my friend on the phone ______.
explanation: Target: just now. Very close to the present moment; just a moment ago.

prompt: Waterbirds, ______ the duck or the gull, are common in the area.
explanation: Target: such as. For example.
